summary: Backend-only regression for the new admin moderation API. All 30 pytest cases (20 original /api/scores + 10 new /api/admin/scores) PASS in 2.31s against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Verified the security model end-to-end (no token / wrong Bearer / Basic scheme all 401 with WWW-Authenticate: Bearer; correct Bearer 200), the functional behaviours (newest-first sort verified via curl on actual timestamps, case-insensitive regex verified by passing lowercase 'neo' which matched both NEO and NEOTRIM rows, limit=9999 capped server-side), the round-trip lifecycle (POST /api/scores -> admin GET filter -> admin DELETE returns {deleted:id, ok:true} -> repeat DELETE returns 404 idempotently), and confirmed hmac.compare_digest is wired into require_admin (server.py:157) — constant-time compare, not == . Public POST /api/scores still works for anonymous users (no token required) — name normalization, score validation, sorting, rank, and _id-leak guards all green. Cleaned up 19 pollution rows (TEST*/HCKER*/NEOTRIM*/ADMINFILTER/DELME/NEO/ABCDEFGHIJKL) via the new DELETE endpoint at end of run; only 2 legitimate rows remain (MORPHEUS 1, TRINITY).
critical_code_review_comments: Security: require_admin in server.py uses hmac.compare_digest on UTF-8 encoded bytes — confirmed constant-time. Returns 401 with WWW-Authenticate: Bearer for both 'missing' and 'invalid' branches (good — does not leak which branch was hit). HTTPBearer(auto_error=False) is correct so we can craft our own 401 response instead of FastAPI's default 403.

Security: when ADMIN_TOKEN env var is empty/missing the dependency returns 503 instead of allowing access — fail-closed posture is correct. Recommend documenting this in operational notes (currently only in test_credentials.md).

Robustness: admin_list_scores clamps limit to [1, 500] and excludes _id — consistent with the public list endpoint. Newest-first sort uses the ISO-8601 string timestamp field; lexicographic sort works correctly here because timestamps are stored in canonical UTC ISO format with fixed width — verified empirically.

Robustness: name regex filter passes the user input straight into a Mongo $regex without escaping. Today only admins (Bearer) can hit it so this is acceptable, but if an admin accidentally pastes a string containing regex metacharacters (e.g. '.+' or '(') they'll get either over-matching or a Mongo error 51091. Suggest re.escape() before passing to $regex for predictable substring semantics.

Idempotency: DELETE returns 404 on second call (verified). Some style guides prefer DELETE to be 200/204 on both calls (truly idempotent). Current behaviour matches the test contract though, so leaving it.
Minor: ADMIN_TOKEN is loaded at module import time. If it is rotated, the backend must be restarted (already documented in test_credentials.md). Not a bug.

Test hygiene: test_admin_list_filter_by_name leaves an 'ADMINFILTER' row in the DB (no teardown). Recommend adding an autouse cleanup fixture that deletes any rows POSTed during a test via the new admin DELETE endpoint, so the suite is fully self-cleaning. (I cleaned manually this run.)

Test coverage gap (non-blocking): no test asserts the actual newest-first ordering — the suite only checks status/array shape. The sort property was verified manually via curl this run. Adding a test that POSTs N rows and asserts timestamp[i] >= timestamp[i+1] would close this gap.

success_rate.frontend: skipped (review request stated frontend was not modified)

seed_data_creation: None. Test fixtures created and immediately cleaned up. Post-run leaderboard contains only 2 legitimate non-test rows: MORPHEUS 1 and TRINITY.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin moderation API is GREEN. Bearer token auth uses hmac.compare_digest (server.py:157). Endpoints: GET /api/admin/scores?limit=&name=&min_score= (newest-first, limit clamped 1..500, name is case-insensitive Mongo $regex on uppercased pattern), DELETE /api/admin/scores/{score_id} returns {deleted, ok:true} or 404. Test files: /app/backend/tests/test_admin_endpoints.py (10 tests) + /app/backend/tests/test_gravshift_api.py (20 tests). Don't pollute leaderboard with TEST*/HCKER*/NEOTRIM* entries — clean up via DELETE if you create any. Suggested unique non-pattern names: ADMINFILTER, DELME, ADM123. Existing legitimate rows to preserve: MORPHEUS 1, TRINITY.
rca of the issue: N/A — no issues found. New admin endpoints implemented correctly with constant-time token compare, fail-closed missing-token behaviour, proper WWW-Authenticate header, and clean separation from the public score-submission flow.
